title: Acronis Assets Manifest
description: Pack manifest for visual assets. DTCG-divergent: borrows $-prefixed vocabulary, project fields live at top-level keys. See ../context/spec.md.

$schema.description: Schema discriminator. Identifies this file as an Acronis assets pack manifest.

name.description: Pack id. MUST equal the manifest filename stem (e.g., icons-stroke-mono.json -> "icons-stroke-mono").

$type.description: Pack-level type. Inherits to every asset unless an asset overrides it (R8).

values.description: Pack-level variant map. Exactly one entry carries `"default": true` — the pack-wide canonical variant. Other entries are shared variant values (computed or source) applied to every asset that does not override per-key.

assets.description: Flat map of asset id -> Asset. Categorization lives in metadata, not in structure. Asset ids MAY start with a digit (illustrations pack uses 4-digit catalog ids).

Values.description: Variant map shared by pack and asset level: variant id -> VariantValue. The canonical variant is the one entry carrying `"default": true`. Pack and asset levels are merged per key (asset wins) before resolution; cardinality of the `default` flag and existence of the canonical source are runtime invariants (see ../context/spec.md).

$type.description: Optional override of the inherited pack-level $type. Used for e.g. a single raster asset inside a vector pack (R8).

$description.description: One-line human summary of the asset.

values.description: Asset's variant map. Entries override pack-supplied values per key. Use null on a key to opt out of an inherited pack value. The asset supplies its canonical's `$file` here; it MAY carry `"default": true` on a different entry to override the pack-level canonical (R12).

allOf.description: Invariant 5 (partial, per-asset $type): when an asset declares its own `$type`, every $file in its values map MUST carry the matching extension (svg for vector, png/webp for raster). Assets that omit `$type` and inherit from the pack are not constrained by this rule.

Platforms.description: Platform scope: which consumers this Asset targets. Per-Asset only; not defaultable. Default value ["PD"]; widen to ["WEB"] only after audit.

Metadata.description: Per-Asset descriptive fields. Per-Asset only -- not defaultable, not per-Variant.

VariantValue.description: A variant entry resolves to exactly one of: null (opt-out -- this variant does not exist for this asset), a SourceValue (own binary and/or canonical marker), or a ComputedValue (computed from a sibling variant by applying rules). The three branches are key-disjoint, so the oneOf is unambiguous.

$file.description: Project-relative path to the on-disk binary. MUST resolve under ./packs/<pack>/. File-extension / $type consistency (svg for vector, png|webp for raster) is a runtime invariant, not enforced by the schema.

default.description: Marks this variant as the canonical. Exactly one entry in a pack's `values` carries it (pack-wide canonical); an asset MAY carry it on a different entry to override the pack canonical (R12). The canonical is always a source, never computed. At pack level the canonical entry is `{ "default": true }` with no `$file` (each asset supplies the binary); at asset level it is `{ "$file": ..., "default": true }`. The anyOf requires at least one of `$file`/`default`, so an empty `{}` is rejected.

$from.description: OPTIONAL source sibling for this computation: a literal variant id (e.g., "24", "48", "dark") looked up on the same asset. OMIT it to compute from the effective canonical -- the common case (R1). Present only to derive from a NON-canonical sibling (R9).

$rules.description: Ordered list of rule ids (declared under ./rules/) to apply, left to right, to the resolved source (the `$from` sibling, or the effective canonical when `$from` is omitted).

com.figma.nodeId.description: Figma node identifier carried through for round-trip back into Figma.

com.figma.variableId.description: Figma variable identifier carried through for round-trip back into Figma.

Extensions.description: Open object accepting any reverse-DNS-namespaced key (e.g., com.figma.*, com.acronis.*, io.example.*). propertyNames enforces multi-segment rDNS shape; additionalProperties: true keeps the spec extensible without schema changes.
